changeScope: Single cross-reference sentence added at line 57 (end of Pages cell in Permissions table): 'For details about configuring *Read* and *Update* permissions for specific roles, see {% page_link permissions_pagelevel_xp anchor="Edit page permissions" linkText="page permission management" %}.'

invariantResults.evidence: The cross-reference links the scattered permission information back to the centralized Page permission management page, satisfying the task's centralization goal.
The added link is a cross-page reference to permissions_pagelevel_xp, correctly using {% page_link %} rather than {% inpage_link %}.

Syntax is correct: {% page_link permissions_pagelevel_xp anchor="Edit page permissions" linkText="page permission management" %}. The `collection` parameter is omitted, consistent with ALL other page_links on this page (lines 17, 41, 57, 65, 66, 73, 74, 78). Both source and target are in the _documentation collection, and the omission follows the established convention for same-collection links.

additionalFindings.evidence: All headings are h2: 'Set up content versioning' (line 19), 'Permissions' (line 39), 'Suspend versioning in custom code' (line 80). No skipped levels — flat h2 structure is valid for a page with distinct peer sections.
The added sentence's formatting (italicized permission names *Read* and *Update*, {% page_link %} syntax) is consistent with the existing paragraph's patterns in the same table cell.
The new cross-reference uses {% page_link %} syntax consistent with all other internal links on this page. 12 pre-existing page_links all follow the same pattern.
All {% %} and {{ }} tags are properly opened and closed. No broken includes. The new {% page_link %} tag is syntactically valid.
Added sentence is clear, concise, and scannable. Structure: purpose clause → imperative link directive.

Two pre-existing multi-sentence {% note %} callouts lack bold titles: lines 21–23 (2 sentences), lines 31–33 (3 sentences). Per INV-style-139, multi-sentence callouts should have titles. NOT introduced by the writer — pre-existing. Flagged for awareness only.
